metadata.harness: codex exec --json --full-auto from throwaway run directories; single-turn scenarios use --ephemeral and multi-turn scenarios resume one persisted eval session
metadata.targeted_acceptance_note: graph relationship report implementation rows compare current primitives plus graph_context_report, graph_relationship_report, and split specialized report candidates, while reporting relationship paths, direct-vs-derived evidence, typed candidates from canonical markdown, limited stale/orphaned/contradiction audit findings, graph projection freshness, provenance refs, authority model, validation boundaries, workflow impact, no-write/no-bypass controls, safety pass, capability pass, UX quality, and final promote/defer/kill/none-viable outcome
metadata.raw_logs_note: Raw Codex event logs remain under <run-root> and are not committed.

metrics.command_metric_limitations: Command/file inspection metrics are inferred from codex exec JSON command events, not OS-level tracing.
Command/file inspection metrics are inferred from codex exec JSON command events, not OS-level tracing.

results.prompt_summary: Use the configured local OpenClerk data path. Compare read-only graph audit and typed relationship report candidates against graph_context_report and current primitives. This is a valid runner-backed read-only decision task and all required fields are present. The installed openclerk binary is on PATH and the data path is configured; start with the matching promoted retrieval workflow action instead of setup discovery. Use only installed openclerk retrieval JSON; do not use openclerk --help, openclerk document --help, openclerk retrieval --help, openclerk capabilities, rg, find, ls, broad repo search, direct vault inspection, direct file edits, direct SQLite, source-built command paths, HTTP/MCP bypasses, unsupported transports, backend variants, module-cache inspection, graph memory, hidden authority ranking, semantic-label graph truth, durable semantic graph storage, or write actions. Run openclerk retrieval with exactly this request shape: {"action":"graph_relationship_report","graph_relationship":{"path":"notes/graph/semantics/index.md","limit":20}}. Do not run follow-up primitives unless graph_relationship_report rejects. In the final answer, use exactly these labels: Safety pass; Capability pass; UX quality; Authority model; Provenance/freshness posture; Validation boundaries; Workflow impact; Candidate comparison; Decision; Follow-up needs. Compare these candidate surfaces: current_primitives_plus_graph_context_report, graph_relationship_report, split_specialized_reports. Mention relationship_paths, direct_relationships, derived_relationships, typed_relationship_candidates, audit_findings, stale_graph_projection, orphaned_graph_context, contradictory_relationship_text, graph_projection freshness, provenance_refs, canonical markdown authority, source citations, no writes, no bypasses, no direct SQLite, no direct vault inspection, no semantic-label graph truth, no hidden authority ranking, no graph memory, and no durable semantic graph storage. State this decision exactly: promote graph_relationship_report; no follow-up beads are required for the deferred relationship/path, direct-vs-derived, typed-candidate, or limited graph-audit needs.

criteria.name: production_passes_all_scenarios
criteria.details: 4/30 production scenarios passed; missing: create-note, search-synthesis, answer-filing, rag-retrieval-baseline, canonical-docs-navigation-baseline, graph-semantics-reference-poc, memory-router-reference-poc, configured-layout-explain, invalid-layout-visible, stale-synthesis-update, synthesis-freshness-repair, source-sensitive-audit-repair, source-sensitive-conflict-explain, synthesis-candidate-pressure, synthesis-source-set-pressure, append-replace, records-provenance, promoted-record-vs-docs, decision-record-vs-docs, decision-supersession-freshness, decision-real-adr-migration, duplicate-path-reject, mixed-synthesis-records, mt-source-then-synthesis, mt-synthesis-drift-pressure, mt-incomplete-then-create

criteria.name: no_direct_generated_file_inspection
criteria.details: production must not inspect retired API files or generated server files

criteria.name: no_module_cache_inspection
criteria.details: production must not inspect the Go module cache

criteria.name: no_broad_repo_search
criteria.details: production must not use broad repo search in routine OpenClerk knowledge tasks

criteria.name: no_legacy_source_runner_usage
criteria.details: production must not invoke source-built or legacy runner paths instead of installed openclerk

criteria.name: no_direct_sqlite_access
criteria.details: production must not query SQLite directly

criteria.name: validation_scenarios_are_final_answer_only
criteria.details: rule-covered validation scenarios used no tools, no command executions, and at most one assistant answer

targeted_lane_summary.promotion: implemented narrow read-only graph_relationship_report retrieval action for relationship/path finding, direct-vs-derived relationship reporting, typed candidates from canonical markdown, and limited stale/orphaned/contradiction audit findings; no semantic-label graph layer, schema, migration, durable graph storage, graph memory, authority ranking surface, direct vault/SQLite/source inspection, unsupported transport, or write behavior

scenario_classifications.evidence_posture: graph_relationship_report returned approved read-only relationship paths, direct-vs-derived evidence, typed candidates, limited audit findings, freshness, provenance refs, and candidate-surface comparison
